questions.q: In which year was the Hindi film Do Bigha Zamin released?
questions.explanation: Bimal Roy's Do Bigha Zamin was released in 1953.

questions.q: Who directed the 1953 film Do Bigha Zamin?
questions.explanation: Do Bigha Zamin was directed by Bimal Roy.

questions.q: Which actor played the lead role of the farmer Shambhu Mahato in Do Bigha Zamin (1953)?
questions.explanation: Balraj Sahni played the farmer Shambhu Mahato in Do Bigha Zamin.

questions.q: What does the title Do Bigha Zamin translate to in English?
questions.explanation: Do Bigha Zamin means 'Two Acres of Land', referring to the patch of land the farmer fights to keep.

questions.q: To which city does the farmer Shambhu migrate to earn money in Do Bigha Zamin (1953)?
questions.explanation: Shambhu migrates to Calcutta to earn money and save his land.

questions.q: What job does the farmer Shambhu take up in the city in Do Bigha Zamin (1953)?
questions.explanation: In Calcutta, Shambhu works as a hand-pulled rickshaw-puller to raise money.

questions.q: Why does Shambhu need to raise money in Do Bigha Zamin (1953)?
options: To pay for his son's education
To buy a new pair of bullocks
To repay a debt and save his two acres of land
questions.explanation: Shambhu must repay a debt to the landlord to avoid losing his two acres of ancestral land.

questions.q: The 1953 film Do Bigha Zamin is widely regarded as a landmark of which film movement?
questions.explanation: Do Bigha Zamin became a template for India's parallel cinema movement of realist, socially conscious films.

questions.q: Which European film movement strongly influenced the style of Do Bigha Zamin (1953)?
questions.explanation: Do Bigha Zamin was heavily influenced by Italian neorealism, especially films like Bicycle Thieves.

questions.q: Do Bigha Zamin (1953) is set against the backdrop of which kind of community's struggles?
options: Rural farmers and the landless poor
questions.explanation: The film centres on the hardships of a poor rural farmer and the exploitation of the landless by a landlord.

questions.q: Which composer wrote the music for Do Bigha Zamin (1953)?
questions.explanation: The music for Do Bigha Zamin was composed by Salil Chowdhury, who also wrote the original story.

questions.q: Who wrote the original story on which Do Bigha Zamin (1953) was based?
questions.explanation: The film was based on Salil Chowdhury's story 'Rickshawalla'; Chowdhury also composed the film's music.

questions.q: The title and theme of Do Bigha Zamin were inspired by a poem by which Bengali writer?
questions.explanation: The film drew inspiration from Rabindranath Tagore's poem 'Dui Bigha Jomi'.

questions.q: Which actress played Parvati (Paro), the farmer's wife, in Do Bigha Zamin (1953)?
questions.explanation: Nirupa Roy played Parvati, Shambhu's wife, in Do Bigha Zamin.

questions.q: Who wrote the lyrics for the songs of Do Bigha Zamin (1953)?
questions.explanation: The lyrics for Do Bigha Zamin were penned by Shailendra.

questions.q: Which song from Do Bigha Zamin (1953), evoking the monsoon and rural hope, became especially well known?
questions.explanation: 'Hariyala Sawan Dhol Bajata Aaya' is among the best-remembered songs from Do Bigha Zamin.

questions.q: Under which production banner was Do Bigha Zamin (1953) made?
questions.explanation: Do Bigha Zamin was the first film produced under Bimal Roy Productions.

questions.q: Before moving to Bombay and making Do Bigha Zamin, Bimal Roy had trained at which famous Calcutta studio?
questions.explanation: Bimal Roy trained at New Theatres in Calcutta before establishing himself in Bombay cinema.

questions.q: It is widely noted that Balraj Sahni prepared for his role in Do Bigha Zamin (1953) by doing what?
options: Actually pulling a rickshaw on Calcutta's streets to master the part
Studying under a theatre guru in Lucknow
questions.explanation: Balraj Sahni famously trained by pulling a rickshaw on Calcutta's streets to bring authenticity to his role.

questions.q: Which young actor played Kanhaiya, the farmer's son, in Do Bigha Zamin (1953)?
questions.explanation: The child actor Rattan Kumar played Kanhaiya, the son of Shambhu, in Do Bigha Zamin.

questions.q: At the very first National Film Awards (1954, for 1953 films), Do Bigha Zamin received which honour?
options: The All India Certificate of Merit for the best feature film
questions.explanation: Do Bigha Zamin received the All India Certificate of Merit for the best feature film at the inaugural National Film Awards; the President's Gold Medal that year went to the Marathi film Shyamchi Aai.

questions.q: At which international film festival did Do Bigha Zamin win the International Prize (Prix International) in 1954?
questions.explanation: Do Bigha Zamin won the International Prize at the 1954 Cannes Film Festival, the first Indian film to do so.

questions.q: Do Bigha Zamin (1953) also won a prize at which other European festival in 1954?
questions.explanation: Do Bigha Zamin won the Social Progress Award at the 1954 Karlovy Vary International Film Festival, in addition to its Cannes honour.

questions.q: Who served as the cinematographer for Do Bigha Zamin (1953)?
questions.explanation: Kamal Bose was the cinematographer on Do Bigha Zamin, a frequent collaborator of Bimal Roy.

questions.q: Which future acclaimed director worked with Bimal Roy and handled the screenplay and editing of Do Bigha Zamin (1953)?
questions.explanation: Hrishikesh Mukherjee, later a celebrated director, wrote the screenplay and edited Do Bigha Zamin while working with Bimal Roy.

questions.q: Besides Best Film, which second top award did Do Bigha Zamin win at the 1st Filmfare Awards (1954)?
questions.explanation: Do Bigha Zamin won two awards at the 1st Filmfare Awards: Best Film and Best Director (Bimal Roy). Best Actor went to Dilip Kumar (Daag) and Best Music Director to Naushad (Baiju Bawra).

questions.q: Which Italian neorealist film is most often cited as a key influence on Do Bigha Zamin (1953)?
questions.explanation: Vittorio De Sica's Bicycle Thieves is frequently named as the principal neorealist influence on Do Bigha Zamin.

questions.q: In Do Bigha Zamin (1953), what does the landlord ultimately want to do with Shambhu's two acres of land?
options: Convert it into a temple complex
Turn it into a hunting reserve
Build a factory or mill on the acquired land
questions.explanation: The landlord schemes to seize the land to build a mill/factory, driving the central conflict of the film.

questions.q: Besides Do Bigha Zamin, which of these films was also directed by Bimal Roy?
questions.explanation: Bimal Roy directed Madhumati (1958), along with other classics like Devdas and Sujata.

questions.q: Do Bigha Zamin (1953) is historically significant as one of the first commercially notable Hindi films to do what?
options: Combine realist social themes with mainstream success and international acclaim
questions.explanation: Do Bigha Zamin pioneered the blending of serious social realism with mainstream appeal and international recognition in Hindi cinema.
